signals.title: CHF/JPY — G10 Bilateral Theme + SNB Yield Shift
signals.text: CHF/JPY testing 200.83 as the JPY outperforms the G10 complex (+0.532% avg) while the CHF lags (-0.507% avg). Despite a -75bp carry disadvantage for CHF (SNB 0.0% vs BoJ 0.75%), the pair remains under pressure as SNB 10Y yields rise to 0.481% and sight deposit rates hold at 0.0%/-0.25%. JPY strength is supported by a positive Economic Surprise Index (net +16), suggesting data outperformance is countering the carry deficit. The 1.038% G10 spread confirms a dominant bilateral divergence.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Retail vs Institutional Divergence + BoC
signals.text: USD/CAD at 1.3931 showing acute positioning divergence: retail is 87% short (extreme contrarian long signal) while COT speculators remain net short -49,052. With the Fed at 3.75% vs BoC 2.25% (+150bp USD carry advantage) and WTI Oil rising +3.63% to $93.8, the CAD faces conflicting drivers. Retail capitulation risk rises if the pair holds above 1.4000 following the BoC press conference. Watch Governor Macklem's stance on the 0.17% real rate for direction.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Volatility Premium + Positioning
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1541 exhibits an elevated Vol Risk Premium (VRP) of +3.0% (IV 7.1% vs HV30 4.1%), signaling heavy institutional hedging demand ahead of the June 11 ECB meeting. COT LF net short -22,320 aligns with the -175bp carry disadvantage (Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2.0%). Options market 25d RR at -0.36 confirms a mild put skew, suggesting the market is paying for downside protection despite the 1D +0.12% price bounce. Sustained move below 1.1500 targets the 1M low.

signals.title: NZD/USD — Retail Extreme + Real Rate Headwind
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5832 faces a structural headwind with a -0.83% real policy rate (2.25% nominal vs 3.08% inflation expectations). Retail sentiment is at a 91% long extreme, directly fading the COT LF net short of -27,797. This 15.1% long institutional positioning suggests a crowded retail trade vulnerable to liquidation. The +150bp USD carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs RBNZ 2.25%) continues to anchor the pair's downside bias. Watch for a break of 0.5800 to trigger retail stops.
